verbatim_text: Actually, I don't have the Gemini plot here. But Stewardy Nano and Gemini, there's a reasonable gap there. So I think the difference is that here we measure it on harder problems. There I had this aggregate which was measuring on a larger subset, which is considered easier proof problems too. But here, this gain is significant because this is on much harder test problems that are pulled out. So we made sure that these are very different from what kind of proof these models are trained on. So this gain from 40 to 60 here is big. And that difference between 54 and 58, it looks pretty small. But that 4% is on a big fraction of hard problems, which is reflected here sometimes. Does that answer your question? Cool. So we saw this result wherein we can scale test time adaptation and match Gemini 3. And this was an open source collaboration with folks at Hugging Face, Numina Math, ETH Zurich, and CMU. And our work was awarded the Lord Slingshot grant as well.
